2007-04-01.title: Japan’s Textbooks Reflect Revised History
2007-04-01.abstract: New high school textbooks will no longer acknowledge that the Imperial Army was responsible for a major atrocity in Okinawa.

2007-04-01.title: Baron Freytag von Loringhoven, Aide to Hitler, Dies at 93
2007-04-01.abstract: As a young German Army officer, Baron Bernd Freytag von Loringhoven was one of the last to flee Hitler’s Berlin bunker as Soviet troops closed in.

2007-04-01.title: Palestinian Faction Aims to Unite Its Militias
2007-04-01.abstract: The move is an apparent effort to strengthen and impose discipline on its notoriously divided militias.

2007-04-01.title: 25 Years After War, Wealth Changes Falklands
2007-04-01.abstract: The British-controlled islands in the South Atlantic are facing an influx of outsiders attracted by new prosperity.

2007-04-01.title: Australian Critics See Politics in Detainee Deal
2007-04-01.abstract: Prime Minister John Howard is facing accusations that the relatively brief sentence on Guantánamo detainee David Hicks resulted from governmental pressure.

2007-04-02.title: Wedged Amid African Crises, a Neglected Nation Suffers
2007-04-02.abstract: The region around the Central African Republic has spawned one of the world’s deadliest and most complex crises.

2007-04-02.title: Tsunami Swamps South Pacific Islands
2007-04-02.abstract: An undersea earthquake sent huge tides rolling across low-lying areas of the Solomon Islands, inundating entire villages and causing an unknown number of deaths.

2007-04-02.title: Gaza Protest Demands Action on Kindapped Journalist
2007-04-02.abstract: More than a hundred Palestinian journalists and other civilians today demanded greater efforts to find and free Alan Johnston, the BBC correspondent in Gaza.

2007-04-02.title: Iran-Britain Standoff Shows Hints of Easing
2007-04-02.abstract: Iran’s chief negotiator said today that “there is no need for a trial” for the 15 captured British naval personnel.

2007-04-02.title: Iraq Town Struggles to Recover After Most Residents Fled
2007-04-02.abstract: The demise of Saba al Bor is a lesson in how quickly things can change for the worse in Iraq and how difficult it has been to make things better.

2007-04-03.title: Pelosi Arrives in Damascus for Syria Talks
2007-04-03.abstract: Nancy Pelosi, the Democratic Speaker of the House, arrived in Damascus, the capital of Syria, today for a controversial visit that is seen as part of an attempt to sway American policy in the Middle East.

2007-04-03.title: Rebel Ambush in Darfur Kills 5 African Union Peacekeepers in Deadliest Attack on the Force
2007-04-03.abstract: The latest attack will likely increase pressure on the African Union to either strengthen the small, under-equipped force or withdraw.

2007-04-03.title: Blair Sees Iran Standoff at ‘Fairly Critical’ Stage
2007-04-03.abstract: Prime minister Tony Blair of Britain said today that the release of the 15 captured British sailors could still be secured by diplomatic efforts.

2007-04-03.title: Many Lawmakers Go to Iraq, but Few Change Their Minds
2007-04-03.abstract: While lawmakers say they are moved by their experiences, they almost always return with their convictions firmly reinforced.

2007-04-03.title: U.S. Cites Indian Government Agencies in Weapons Conspiracy
2007-04-03.abstract: An indictment suggests that India broke a pledge not to flout export laws and seek sensitive weapons technology from the U.S.

2007-04-04.title: Captors Release Kidnapped Iranian Diplomat in Baghdad
2007-04-04.abstract: Iraq’s foreign minister said that the freeing of the diplomat was unrelated to the negotiations over the 15 British sailors and marines seized by Iran.

2007-04-04.title: A New Decree From Chávez: Less Elbow-Bending
2007-04-04.abstract: A decree by President Hugo Chávez severely limiting alcohol sales in Venezuela for much of Holy Week has generated a public outcry.

2007-04-04.title: Pelosi, Warmly Greeted in Syria, Is Criticized by White House
2007-04-04.abstract: House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s visit is seen as part of an attempt to sway Bush administration policy on Iraq and the rest of the Middle East.

2007-04-04.title: Lawmakers Who Visited Baghdad Talk of Progress, but Also Danger
2007-04-04.abstract: One Republican congressman said a brief tour of a market had given him some hope that new military tactics were having a measurable impact on security in the city.

2007-04-04.title: Bush Blames Democrats for Impasse Over Iraq Bills
2007-04-04.abstract: The president said Congressional Democrats had “undercut the troops” by passing legislation that ties war financing to the withdrawal of combat units.

2007-04-05.title: Editor of Playboy Indonesia Is Acquitted
2007-04-05.abstract: Hundreds of conservative Muslims staged a protest outside the courtroom during the verdict.

2007-04-05.title: Irish Inquiry Faults Handling of Day of Attacks in 1974
2007-04-05.abstract: The report criticized the police for losing files and documentation, saying documentation had been handled in a way that was “manifestly inappropriate.”

2007-04-05.title: Iran Sets Free 15 Britons Seized at Sea
2007-04-05.abstract: British sailors and marines will arrive in London today on a commercial flight from Tehran, resolving a diplomatic impasse that began on March 23.

2007-04-05.title: Israel’s Protests Are Said to Stall Gulf Arms Sale
2007-04-05.abstract: An arms-sale package that the U.S. is planning to offer Persian Gulf allies has been delayed because of objections from Israel.

2007-04-05.title: U.N. Draft Cites Humans in Current Effects of Climate Shift
2007-04-05.abstract: The U.N. report found that global warming caused by humans has contributed to recent shifts in ecosystems.

2007-04-06.title: 3 Are Charged With Aiding London Transit Attack
2007-04-06.abstract: The chief of counterterrorism at Scotland Yard said that the police now had a better picture of the preparations for the suicide mission.

2007-04-06.title: British Envoy Seeks to Free Reporter Seized in Gaza
2007-04-06.abstract: A senior British diplomat met with the Palestinian prime minister in Gaza to discuss the fate of a kidnapped BBC correspondent.

2007-04-06.title: Greece: Tourists Evacuated From Cruise Ship
2007-04-06.abstract: Four warships and two military helicopters joined a fleet of commercial vessels that rushed to evacuate 1,200 passengers, mostly Americans, and 300 crew members from a cruise ship that ran aground off the Aegean island of Santorini while trying to dock. The authorities said the cruise ship, the Sea Diamond, operated by Louis Hellenic Cruise Lines, began listing after striking a bed of rocks about a mile from the coast. The ship was later towed from the rocks. It had left Greece’s main port of Pireaus on Monday for a weeklong island-hopping cruise in the Aegean.

2007-04-06.title: French Presidential Candidate Skips Visit as Protesters Gather
2007-04-06.abstract: Nicolas Sarkozy, who has had a difficult relationship with France’s ethnic populations, canceled a campaign visit to Lyon after demonstrators staged a raucous protest.

2007-04-06.title: Freed Sailors and Marines: ‘We All Wanted to Get Home’
2007-04-06.abstract: Following is the statement read alternately by Capt. Chris Air, 25, of the Royal Marines, and Lt. Felix Carman, 26, of the Royal Navy, on behalf of the 15 British sailors and marines freed by Iran on Thursday.

2007-04-07.title: Even as Africa Hungers, Policy Slows Delivery of U.S. Food Aid
2007-04-07.abstract: Groups with deep stakes in the current system oppose a proposal that could deliver food aid faster in countries like Zambia, where rations are at risk of running out.

2007-04-07.title: Money Shift Could Clear Way to Shut North Korea Reactor
2007-04-07.abstract: The United States said that it had found a way to return frozen money to North Korea, leaving the North only a week to meet the deadline for disabling its main nuclear facility.

2007-04-07.title: Greece: 2 Missing After Cruise Ship Sinks
2007-04-07.abstract: Divers and the Coast Guard searched for two French tourists, a 45-year-old man and his 16-year-old daughter, reported missing after hundreds of other tourists, including more than 700 Americans, were safely evacuated after their cruise ship hit rocks while trying to dock at the island of Santorini and issued a distress call on Thursday. The ship, the Sea Diamond, left, operated by Louis Hellenic Cruise Lines, sunk yesterday morning, 15 hours after it began taking on water.

2007-04-07.title: McCain Says He Erred on Iraq Security
2007-04-07.abstract: Senator John McCain said he misspoke when he declared his ability to walk freely around a Baghdad market was a sign of a significant improvement in security in Iraq.

2007-04-07.title: Scientists Detail Climate Changes, Poles to Tropics
2007-04-07.abstract: The earth’s climate and ecosystems are already being shaped by the buildup of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere, the leading panel on the issue said.

2007-04-08.title: In Zimbabwe, Mugabe Critics Face Beatings
2007-04-08.abstract: Critics of President Robert G. Mugabe are being savagely beaten by the scores — and some say by the hundreds.

2007-04-08.title: Kosovo War-Crimes Trial Splits West and Prosecutors
2007-04-08.abstract: A trial at the United Nations tribunal in The Hague has created a stark divide between prosecutors at the tribunal and in Kosovo and diplomats from the United Nations and Western governments.

2007-04-08.title: U.S. Warplanes Attack Shiite Gunmen as Fighting Persists in City South of Baghdad
2007-04-08.abstract: American fighter jets carried out an airstrike against Shiite militiamen in the southern city of Diwaniya on Saturday.

2007-04-08.title: On France’s Far Right, Tweaking an Image
2007-04-08.abstract: Jean-Marie Le Pen, the far-right presidential candidate, made a surprise visit to a troubled, multiethnic suburb of Paris on Friday.

2007-04-08.title: Fans Sour on Sweeter Version of Asia’s Smelliest Fruit
2007-04-08.abstract: There is probably no other fruit that elicits such passion — and revulsion — as the durian, possibly the world’s smelliest fruit.

2007-04-09.title: The man (and his dad)!
2007-04-09.abstract: What more can be said? As huge as it was for Fernando Alonso to win in only his second race with his new team, Lewis Hamilton’s continued assault on rookie records in his first two races was mind blowing. The way he held off Massa looked like Michael Schumacher at the end of his career.

2007-04-09.title: 6 Canadians Killed by Afghan Bomb
2007-04-09.abstract: The Canadians were NATO soldiers who were killed when a roadside bomb exploded near their vehicle west of Kandahar City.

2007-04-09.title: Patterns of War Shift in Iraq Amid U.S. Buildup
2007-04-09.abstract: There is little sign that the security push in Baghdad is accomplishing its main purpose: creating an island of stability for Iraqis.

2007-04-09.title: Britons Freed by Iran May Sell Their Stories
2007-04-09.abstract: Some of the 15 sailors and marines who had been captured and released by Iran will be permitted to offer their experiences for sale to the media.

2007-04-09.title: Divided and Embattled East Timor to Elect a President
2007-04-09.abstract: The presidential election has exposed bitter personal rivalries as well as divisions among clans and regions.

2007-04-10.title: Huge Protest in Iraq Demands U.S. Withdraw
2007-04-10.abstract: Tens of thousands of protesters loyal to Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr demanded an end to the American military presence in Iraq.

2007-04-10.title: Mozambique: 139,000 H.I.V. Babies in 6 Months
2007-04-10.abstract: Women with AIDS delivered 139,000 babies infected with H.I.V., the virus that causes AIDS, in the last six months, Unicef reported. The agency also said that 146,000 women carrying the virus had been given drugs to prevent its transmission to their babies. Nearly one in six Mozambican adults carries the virus. The Health Ministry estimates that 20,000 children under the age of 5 died of AIDS last year.

2007-04-10.title: At the Local Abbey, Singing Unto the Lord an Old Song
2007-04-10.abstract: Tourists flock to an 11th-century monastery to hear Gregorian chant, bringing unwanted fame to the reclusive monks.

2007-04-10.title: Macao ready to Release Funds Linked to North Korea, U.S. Says
2007-04-10.abstract: The Macao government is prepared to release $25 million in funds linked to North Korea, the Treasury Department said today.

2007-04-10.title: Fierce Fighting Jolts Baghdad Neighborhood
2007-04-10.abstract: There were conflicting reports about the death toll after some of the worst fighting since the new Baghdad security plan began two month ago.

2007-04-11.title: U.S. Says It Found Iranian Arms in Sunni Area
2007-04-11.abstract: Arms that American military officials say appear to have been manufactured in Iran as recently as last year have turned up in the past week in a Sunni-majority area.

2007-04-11.title: North Korea Offers to Admit Nuclear Inspectors
2007-04-11.abstract: It would be the first time in more than four years that North Korea has allowed U.N. nuclear inspectors.

2007-04-11.title: Bush Criticizes Democrats for Delay in Iraq Spending Bill
2007-04-11.abstract: President Bush accused Democrats of behaving irresponsibly as the two sides moved closer to confrontation over withdrawing troops from Iraq.

2007-04-11.title: Jagjit Singh Chauhan, Sikh Militant Leader in India, Dies at 80
2007-04-11.abstract: Mr. Chauhan began a campaign to create an independent state of Sikhs and was charged with sedition and other crimes in connection with his activities.

2007-04-11.title: The Money Trail That Linked North Korea to Macao
2007-04-11.abstract: Sanctions that became a key sticking point in efforts to dismantle North Korea’s nuclear weapons program began after an elaborate smuggling investigation.

2007-04-12.title: World Bank Chief Apologizes for Arranging Job
2007-04-12.abstract: Paul D. Wolfowitz apologized for his role in arranging a highly paid job at the State Department for a woman with whom he has a personal relationship.

2007-04-12.title: About Creation, Pope Melds Faith With Science
2007-04-12.abstract: Science cannot fully explain the mystery of creation, Pope Benedict XVI said, but he did not reject evolutionary theory.

2007-04-12.title: Chinese Leader Address Japan’s Parliament
2007-04-12.abstract: Prime Minister Wen Jiabao of China today pledged friendship with Japan, but also cautioned that Tokyo must match its past apologies for its wartime aggression with concrete actions.

2007-04-12.title: Allies Meet to Discuss Afghan War, as Taliban Offensive Looms
2007-04-12.abstract: Critics of the Bush administration say that the focus on the Iraq war has jeopardized the early military victory in Afghanistan in 2001.

2007-04-12.title: Italy Calls for Standards in Dealing With Kidnappings
2007-04-12.abstract: Italy proposed international standards for confronting the rising number of high-profile kidnappings in war zones.

2007-04-13.title: Longer Duty Leaves Forces Disappointed but Stoic
2007-04-13.abstract: Most active duty units in Iraq and Afghanistan will serve for 15 months, rather than 12.

2007-04-13.title: Turmoil Grows for Wolfowitz at World Bank
2007-04-13.abstract: Paul D. Wolfowitz apologized for his role in the raise and transfer of his companion, but was greeted by booing.

2007-04-13.title: Defense Chiefs of NATO and Other Allies to Press for More Trainers for Afghan Forces
2007-04-13.abstract: Defense ministers from NATO nations and other allies agreed to press for a new deployment of 3,400 trainers for the Afghan police and armed forces.

2007-04-13.title: U.S. Insists It’s Trying to Free New Jersey Man Jailed in Ethiopia
2007-04-13.abstract: American officials said that they were working for the release of Amir Mohamed Meshal, an American who had been jailed in Ethiopia on suspicion of terrorist activities.

2007-04-13.title: Action on Troops Reshapes Bush’s Debate With Congress
2007-04-13.abstract: Democrats have seized on the announcement of troop extensions as evidence that the president and his inner circle are in denial about what is going on in Iraq.

2007-04-14.title: Markets Suffer After Russia Bans Immigrant Vendors
2007-04-14.abstract: A strict anti-immigrant measure allowing only Russians to sell vegetables and other market goods is resulting in acute shortages and price increases in some regions.

2007-04-14.title: Rights Groups in Zimbabwe Tally Attacks on Opposition
2007-04-14.abstract: Human rights groups in Zimbabwe have begun to document a campaign of abductions and beatings aimed at critics of President Robert G. Mugabe.

2007-04-14.title: Exile Is Rebuked for Urging Ouster of Putin
2007-04-14.abstract: Boris A. Berezovsky, a self-exiled Russian tycoon, earned sharp rebukes from London and Moscow on Friday when he urged the forceful ouster of Vladimir V. Putin as president of Russia and a new revolution there.

2007-04-14.title: A Rebel-Turned-Governor Takes the Wheel in Indonesia
2007-04-14.abstract: Irwandi Yusuf, the new governor of Aceh, has, quite unexpectedly, become the leader of the government he until recently had fought against.

2007-04-14.title: Wolfowitz Fight Has Subplot
2007-04-14.abstract: At its core, the fight is about President Bush and his tumultuous relationship with the rest of the world.

2007-04-15.title: Ethiopia Denies Shipment From Korea Violated Ban
2007-04-15.abstract: The Ethiopian government has denied that it violated United Nations sanctions by carrying out a secret purchase of military equipment from North Korea.

2007-04-15.title: Former Chess Champion Is Arrested at Protest March in Moscow
2007-04-15.abstract: Garry Kasparov, the former chess champion, was arrested during a rally that ended in clashes with riot troops.

2007-04-15.title: Militia Talks Could Reshape Conflict in Darfur
2007-04-15.abstract: Representatives of rebel groups are discussing a military alliance of Arab and non-Arab tribes against the Sudanese government.

2007-04-15.title: Bomber Kills 9 in East Afghanistan
2007-04-15.abstract: The Taliban claimed responsibility for the attack and have vowed to increase their use of suicide attacks.

2007-04-15.title: Suicide Bomber in Karbala Kills 36 Near Bus Terminal
2007-04-15.abstract: A suicide car bomber struck near a bus station in Karbala, a holy city that is a main destination for Shiite pilgrims.

2007-04-16.title: Sudan Drops Objections to U.N. Aid in Darfur
2007-04-16.abstract: The move set the stage for the possible assignment there of United Nations peacekeepers.

2007-04-16.title: Attacks Surge as Iraq Militants Overshadow City
2007-04-16.abstract: Baquba has emerged as a magnet for insurgents and, perhaps, the next major headache for the U.S. military.

2007-04-16.title: Nigeria’s Governing Party Takes Lead in Elections
2007-04-16.abstract: The police said 21 people died in election violence, but newspapers estimated that the toll was at least twice that.

2007-04-16.title: Opposition Rally in Russia Halted by Police for 2nd Day
2007-04-16.abstract: Riot police officers rounded up and arrested protesters and their leaders at an opposition rally in a chaotic street scene.

2007-04-16.title: At a School for the Poor, a Mysterious Illness
2007-04-16.abstract: Mexico’s public health authorities have concluded that the girls are suffering from collective hysteria.

2007-04-17.title: Unusual Trip to Iraq in ’03 for Wolfowitz Companion
2007-04-17.abstract: The Defense Department directed a private contractor to hire the companion of Paul D. Wolfowitz to study issues related to setting up a new government in Iraq.

2007-04-17.title: Gates Will Try to Build Support for Iraqi Premier on Mideast Trip
2007-04-17.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates will urge leaders to back Iraq’s government and Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki.

2007-04-17.title: Bush Leans on Democrats With Talk of Troops Suffering
2007-04-17.abstract: President Bush is calculating that he can weaken the resolve of Democrats with warnings that delays in passing the war spending bill will hurt U.S. troops.

2007-04-17.title: No Spitting on the Road to Olympic Glory, Beijing Says
2007-04-17.abstract: Citywide campaigns are trying to improve the public awareness of manners ahead of the 2008 Summer Olympics.

2007-04-17.title: Britain: Journalists Vote to Boycott Israeli Goods
2007-04-17.abstract: Britain’s biggest journalists union, the 40,000-member National Union of Journalists, has voted in favor of a boycott of Israeli goods, citing what it called Israel’s “military adventures.” The measure, approved by a vote of 66 to 54 at the union’s annual delegates meeting on Friday, called for “a boycott of Israeli goods similar to those boycotts in the struggles against apartheid in South Africa.” The timing was particularly delicate because a BBC journalist, Alan Johnston, has been held for over a month in Gaza. A Palestinian group claimed on Sunday to have killed Mr. Johnston, but the BBC said it was treating the report as a rumor. The union’s Web site says that the delegates criticized Palestinian authorities for failing “to carry out their promises to do all they can to free Alan.” Last year, the largest British university teachers’ association voted to encourage individual academics in Britain to sever professional contact with their counterparts in Israel.

2007-04-18.title: Japanese Researchers Rebut Premier’s Denials on Sex Slavery
2007-04-18.abstract: The group’s public rebuttal was a rare protest in a country where the prime minister’s remarks had stirred little outcry.

2007-04-18.title: Manila Says Peace Corps Worker Is Dead
2007-04-18.abstract: A body believed to be that of an American Peace Corps volunteer was found today in a mountainous province in the north of the Philippines, officials said.

2007-04-18.title: Germany: Army Trainer Dismissed
2007-04-18.abstract: The German Army dismissed an instructor who was filmed in a training exercise telling a recruit to imagine he was firing his machine gun at African-Americans in the Bronx. The Defense Ministry said the instructor, a lance corporal, had been stripped of his rank and pay. The soldier who filmed the scenes with his camera phone is still under investigation.

2007-04-18.title: Army Cost-Cutting in Budget Standoff May Actually Add to Costs
2007-04-18.abstract: Efficiency and economy are likely to be the first casualties in the Army budget standoff between the White House and Congress.

2007-04-18.title: U.S. Missile Deals Bypass, and Annoy, European Union
2007-04-18.abstract: A U.S. proposal to plant antimissile missiles in Poland is prompting European queasiness about American influence on the Continent.

2007-04-19.title: Thailand: Post-Coup Constitution Ready
2007-04-19.abstract: Thailand unveiled a draft constitution that eliminates direct elections for the Senate while bolstering the power of the unelected judiciary. The document, posted on Parliament’s Web site, was drafted by a 35-member committee selected by the junta and will be presented at public forums beginning next week, and then to a national referendum in September. While the 1997 Constitution rescinded by the coup leaders sought to consolidate Thai politics and avoid the fractured coalitions of the 1990s, its proposed replacement seeks to decentralize power by buttressing the power of judges and bureaucrats.

2007-04-19.title: No Solution in Sight as Bush and Lawmakers Discuss Iraq Spending Measure
2007-04-19.abstract: President Bush and Congressional leaders failed to resolve their differences over a timeline for removing most U.S. combat troops from Iraq next year.

2007-04-19.title: Name of High-Pressure Washers Maker Is Drawn Into French Politics
2007-04-19.abstract: Karcher, the company’s brand name is becoming a French political verb, to Karcher. And the context is explosive.

2007-04-19.title: Atomic Agency Confirms Advances by Iran’s Nuclear Program
2007-04-19.abstract: Iran has begun enriching small amounts of uranium at a newly opened underground site at its main nuclear complex.

2007-04-19.title: Gates Reassures Israel About Arms Sales in Gulf
2007-04-19.abstract: The defense secretary said today that arms sales to Persian Gulf countries would not threaten Israeli security.

2007-04-20.title: Gates Assures Israel on Plan to Sell Arms to Saudis
2007-04-20.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said the sale would not threaten Israel’s military superiority and was necessary to counter a threat from Iran.

2007-04-20.title: South Africa: Late Commuters Burn Train
2007-04-20.abstract: Commuters in suburban Pretoria set two train cars on fire after losing patience with efforts to repair a fault that had stopped traffic on the train’s line, the commuter authority Metrorail said. The train had been stopped by a red signal at the Greenview station, but when technicians arrived to make repairs, some furious commuters threw stones at them, injuring two repairmen and three commuters. “Others set the carriages on fire,” the authority’s spokesman, Thokozani Zitha, told the SAPA news service. “We were able to save a third carriage.”.

2007-04-20.title: Wolfowitz Backed Friend for Iraq Contract in ’03
2007-04-20.abstract: An inquiry found that Paul D. Wolfowitz recommended that his companion be awarded a contract for travel to Iraq in 2003.

2007-04-20.title: Leading Democrat in Senate Tells Reporters, ‘This War Is Lost’
2007-04-20.abstract: Senator Harry Reid delivered a biting critique of the Iraq war, saying there was no military solution to the conflict.

2007-04-20.title: Gates Presses Iraq Premier on Healing Sectarian Divide
2007-04-20.abstract: Robert M. Gates’s visit is likely to highlight the starkly different political realities that drive the U.S. and Iraqi governments.

2007-04-21.title: World Bank to Broaden Its Inquiry Into Wolfowitz
2007-04-21.abstract: The board of directors said their inquiry into Paul D. Wolfowitz’s leadership would extend beyond allegations of favoritism.

2007-04-21.title: Pentagon Invites Kremlin to Link Missile Systems
2007-04-21.abstract: The U.S. is offering Russia a new package of incentives to drop its opposition to U.S. missile defense sites in Poland and the Czech Republic.

2007-04-21.title: Fears of Fraud Grow Before Nigerian Vote
2007-04-21.abstract: An opposition party said that soldiers seized ballots that had already been filled out in favor of the governing party’s presidential candidate.

2007-04-21.title: India Debates Its Right to Nuclear Testing
2007-04-21.abstract: Indian officials are arguing whether the limitations of a nuclear accord with the U.S. offend the country’s sense of sovereignty.

2007-04-21.title: A Taliban Leader in Pakistan Says He Would Aid bin Laden
2007-04-21.abstract: A Pakistani Taliban commander in the country’s tribal regions said he would give Osama bin Laden protection if he so desires.

2007-04-22.title: Military Cites ‘Negligence’ in Aftermath of Iraq Killings
2007-04-22.abstract: A military investigation found that the Marine Corps chain of command in Iraq engaged in “willful negligence” in failing to investigate an attack by marines that killed 24 unarmed Iraqis.

2007-04-22.title: Certifying Coffee Aids Farmers and Forests in Chiapas
2007-04-22.abstract: The rush to certify Mexican coffee is drawing an expanding list of players, including giant plantations and multinational traders.

2007-04-22.title: Israeli Minister Steps Aside Pending Inquiry
2007-04-22.abstract: The investigation involves suspicions that Israel’s finance minister embezzled funds before he joined the government, the police said.

2007-04-22.title: Election in Nigeria Sullied by Chaos
2007-04-22.abstract: The problems raised the possibility that the presidential election might not produce a credible winner.

2007-04-22.title: Dozens Killed in Mortar Attack in Somalia
2007-04-22.abstract: In the fourth day of fighting between Islamist insurgents and Ethiopian soldiers, mortar shells caused the exodus from Mogadishu to swell.

2007-04-23.title: Afghan Bombings Kill 9 and Wound More Than 40
2007-04-23.abstract: Two bombings, one of them a suicide attack, rocked the eastern town of Khost on Sunday, killing nine people and wounding more than 40, officials said.

2007-04-23.title: Africa’s Crisis of Democracy
2007-04-23.abstract: Analysts said the Nigerian vote showed that many African voters are steadily losing confidence in their democracies.

2007-04-23.title: Chaos in Somalia as Fighting Intensifies and Death Toll Rises
2007-04-23.abstract: Government forces and insurgents blasted each other for a fifth straight day on Sunday, killing scores of civilians and plunging the country deeper into chaos.

2007-04-23.title: Iraqi Premier Orders Work Stopped on Wall
2007-04-23.abstract: The wall, which would have isolated a Sunni neighborhood, came under criticism from both Sunni Arab and Shiite parties.

2007-04-23.title: U.S. Knew of China’s Missile Test, but Kept Silent
2007-04-23.abstract: Some experts say America might have been able to discourage China from launching the antisatellite missile.

2007-04-24.title: Frustration Over Wall Unites Sunni and Shiite
2007-04-24.abstract: The outcry about the construction of a wall around a Sunni neighborhood has revealed deep Iraqi frustration.

2007-04-24.title: In Twist of History, Kurds Patrol Baghdad
2007-04-24.abstract: American commanders consider Kurdish soldiers to be a critical part of the new Baghdad security plan because of their fighting prowess.

2007-04-24.title: Governing Party Wins in Nigeria, but Many Claim Fraud
2007-04-24.abstract: Umaru Yar’Adua easily won the presidential election in Nigeria, but international observers said the voting was not credible.

2007-04-24.title: South Korea Says Food Aid Depends on North Korea
2007-04-24.abstract: South Korea, facing criticism after it agreed Sunday to send 400,000 tons of rice to North Korea despite the North’s prevarication on a Feb 13 international agreement to shut down its main nuclear reactor, warned that North Korea might not get all the aid if it continued to refuse to live up to its commitment. “We emphasized that the speed and timing of the aid shipments could be changed depending on the North’s sincere implementation of the Feb. 13 agreement,” Lee Jae-Joung, the unification minister, said in a statement. The agreement signed Sunday contained no such preconditions. South Korea is to send the first shipment of rice in late May. Critics from the political opposition and conservative civic groups in Seoul had urged the government not to promise any aid until after the North had made a move to shut down the nuclear reactor.

2007-04-24.title: Boris N. Yeltsin, Reformer Who Broke Up the U.S.S.R., Dies at 76
2007-04-24.abstract: Boris N. Yeltsin, Russia’s first freely elected leader, presided over the end of the Soviet Union and the Communist Party.

2007-04-25.title: ‘Third Man’ in French Election Unleashes Torrent of Criticism
2007-04-25.abstract: François Bayrou then appeared to give an opportunity to Ségolène Royal, the Socialist candidate, to win his support.

2007-04-25.title: In Somalia, Those Who Feed Off Anarchy Fuel It
2007-04-25.abstract: Opportunists who profit from chaos are trying to help bring down the transitional government.

2007-04-25.title: Sri Lanka: Rebels Kill 6 Soldiers in Second Air Raid
2007-04-25.abstract: An air raid by Tamil separatist rebels on a government military base on the northern Jaffna peninsula killed at least six soldiers and wounded 13 others. The military said it averted a larger attack on the base, at Palaly, by firing at the oncoming aircraft. The rebels unveiled their aerial capacity for the first time last month, when they bombed a military base outside the capital, Colombo, and killed three soldiers.

2007-04-25.title: Qaeda-Linked Group Claims Responsibility for Killing of 9 G.I.’s
2007-04-25.abstract: In two Internet postings, the insurgent group, the Islamic State of Iraq, boasted of carrying out the attack, one of the deadliest against American forces in the war.

2007-04-25.title: Canadian Vote Defeats Proposal for Leaving Afghanistan
2007-04-25.abstract: The narrow defeat illustrates the growing demand in Canada for a debate over the country’s role in the United States-led mission in Afghanistan.

2007-04-26.title: Missile-Defense Plan No Threat to Russia, Rice Says
2007-04-26.abstract: The secretary of state’s comments are part of a White House campaign to rally support for the proposal.

2007-04-26.title: Wolfowitz Escalates Battle to Stay at Bank
2007-04-26.abstract: Paul D. Wolfowitz, president of the World Bank, appealed for more time to defend himself against allegations of favoritism and other matters.

2007-04-26.title: Iran: Nuclear Negotiator Meets With Europe’s Chief Diplomat
2007-04-26.abstract: Iran met with the European Union over its uranium enrichment program, the first face-to-face talks between the top negotiators for the sides since the United Nations Security Council expanded sanctions on Iran in March. The talks, in Ankara, Turkey, continued late into the evening. At their conclusion, after five hours, Iran’s nuclear negotiator, Ali Larijani, said, “There are ideas on the table, still in discussion,” the Anatolian news agency reported. “We have time to continue also tomorrow. Within two weeks, we will have more meetings.” Javier Solana, the European Union’s foreign policy chief who is negotiating on behalf of the permanent members of the Security Council, called the talks “productive.” A press conference is scheduled for today.

2007-04-26.title: U.S. Officer in Iraq Accused of  ‘Aiding the Enemy’
2007-04-26.abstract: The charge  is rare and serious, with a maximum sentence of death.

2007-04-26.title: Putin Suspends Arms Pact Over Missile Dispute
2007-04-26.abstract: Russia will suspend its compliance with a treaty on arms in Europe, President Vladimir V. Putin said.

2007-04-27.title: Britain: Army Mulls Future of Prince Harry in Iraq
2007-04-27.abstract: The army said it was reviewing its decision to allow Prince Harry, the younger son of Prince Charles and the third in line for the throne, to serve with his army unit in Iraq. “Prince Harry’s deployment to Iraq is, as we’ve always said, under constant consideration,” a spokeswoman for the Ministry of Defense said. But, she added, “it is still our intent that Harry will deploy as a troop leader.” British news outlets have reported that the Defense Ministry has grown increasingly nervous about the security implications of such a move. Eleven British soldiers have been killed in Iraq in the past month, including two whose armored reconnaissance vehicle — the same kind Harry would be using — was hit by a roadside bomb.

2007-04-27.title: Estonia: Excavation Near Soviet Monument
2007-04-27.abstract: Estonia began excavating the earth surrounding a Soviet-era war memorial in the capital, Tallinn, a step toward relocating it to a military cemetery. The monument, the Bronze Soldier, commemorates the victory over Nazi Germany but is viewed by many Estonians as a symbol of the Soviet occupation that followed. Proposals to remove the monument have brought strong reactions from ethnic Russians in Estonia and from the Russian government.

2007-04-27.title: Saudis Arrest 172 in Anti-Terror Sweep
2007-04-27.abstract: Officials said the suspects were planning to blow up oil installations and attack public officials and military posts.

2007-04-27.title: Russia Rebukes Estonia for Moving Soviet Statue
2007-04-27.abstract: The removal of the monument following a night of violent protests prompted diplomatic fury from Russia.

2007-04-27.title: China Appoints New Foreign Minister
2007-04-27.abstract: BEIJING, April 27 — China appointed Yang Jiechi, a former ambassador to Washington, as its foreign minister, state media reported today, at a time when managing complex relations with the United States over issues like trade, Taiwan and North Korea’s nuclear weapons remains a top priority for the governing Communist Party.

2007-04-28.title: Japan’s Leader Walks Thin Line With Remarks on Sex Slavery
2007-04-28.abstract: TOKYO, April 28 — In his first visit to the United States as Japan’s leader, Prime Minister Shinzo Abe quickly got on a first-name basis with President Bush and secured an invitation to the ranch in Crawford, Tex., for the next time.

2007-04-28.title: Japan Court Rules Against Sex Slaves and Laborers
2007-04-28.abstract: Japan’s highest court denied compensation to wartime sex slaves and forced laborers from China but acknowledged the coercion.

2007-04-28.title: Turkish Military Issues Threat as Voting Is Derailed
2007-04-28.abstract: The military hinted that it might act against the Turkish government if it strayed too far from secularism.

2007-04-28.title: After Violent Night, Estonia Removes a Soviet-Era Memorial
2007-04-28.abstract: The removal of the monument prompted a renewed diplomatic fury in neighboring Russia.

2007-04-28.title: Former C.I.A. Chief’s Memoir Irritates Some High-Ranking Readers
2007-04-28.abstract: George J. Tenet’s account of tensions between intelligence officers and Bush administration policymakers fills in new details, and some of his critics have begun to reply.

2007-04-29.title: Taliban Insurgents Release Hostage After 3 Weeks
2007-04-29.abstract: Taliban insurgents released a French woman aid worker in southern Afghanistan, one of a group of five people taken hostage three weeks ago.

2007-04-29.title: A Saudi Prince Tied to Bush Is Sounding Off-Key
2007-04-29.abstract: Prince Bandar bin Sultan may no longer be an unerring beacon of Saudi intent, U.S. officials say.

2007-04-29.title: Olmert’s Hold on Power Threatened by Report on War
2007-04-29.abstract: An official report on the war in Lebanon is expected to contain harsh criticism of the Israeli prime minister and other officials.

2007-04-29.title: New Conflicts Accompany Nepal’s Efforts at Democracy
2007-04-29.abstract: A year after a return to democratic rule, a useless session of Parliament represents the fissures that have cut through Nepal’s body politic.

2007-04-29.title: Taliban Free Aid Worker, Keeping Four
2007-04-29.abstract: Taliban insurgents released a French female aid worker in southern Afghanistan, one of a group of five people taken hostage three weeks ago.

2007-04-30.title: Afghans Protest After Coalition Raid Kills 6 People
2007-04-30.abstract: A raid by American and Afghan troops on a suspected bomb-making compound before dawn on Sunday killed six people, including two women, and wounded two children.

2007-04-30.title: Iran to Attend Regional Talks on Iraq Violence
2007-04-30.abstract: The stage is set for the first cabinet-level meeting between Iran and the U.S. since the end of 2004.

2007-04-30.title: Rice Rebuts Tenet’s Assertion That ’01 Warning Was Ignored
2007-04-30.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice defended herself against the former C.I.A. director’s assertions that she had dismissed his warnings of possible terrorist attacks.

2007-04-30.title: U.S. Clashes With Militia in Baghdad
2007-04-30.abstract: Militiamen loyal to the Shiite cleric Moktada al-Sadr clashed with American forces in northern Baghdad on Sunday.

2007-04-30.title: Sri Lanka Rebels Bomb Fuel Depot, Closing Airport
2007-04-30.abstract: Tamil rebel warplanes bombed two fuel storage depots early Sunday, tripping defenses that temporarily blacked out the city and closed the airport.
